name: Python
creator: Guido van Rossum
description: Python is a high-level, versatile programming language favored for its readability and ease of use. It is widely used in automation, data science, web, and scripting.
usedFor: Web development, data science, scripting, automation, machine learning.
funFact: Python is named after the British comedy group Monty Python.

terminalCommands.install: Download from python.org or use: pip install

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables are names that store data values. In Python, you don't need to declare the type — it's inferred automatically.

keyTerms.definition: A name attached to a value.
Giving a value to a variable using '='.

commonMistakes: Trying to declare types like in C (not needed).
topics.challenge: Create a variable called greeting with the value 'Welcome'.

topics.title: Lists
topics.explanation: Lists are Python's built-in data structure for holding an ordered sequence of items.

keyTerms.definition: An ordered, mutable collection of items.
The position of an item in a list.

topics.challenge: Make a list of 3 colors and print the last one.

topics.title: Loops
topics.explanation: Loops allow you to repeat a block of code multiple times. The 'for' loop iterates over items.

keyTerms.definition: Repeats code for each item in a sequence.
One round through a loop.

topics.challenge: Print 'Hello' five times using a loop.
topics.challengeHint: Use range(5) to loop five times.

topics.title: Functions
topics.explanation: Functions in Python group code into reusable blocks. Use 'def' to define one.

keyTerms.definition: A block of code that can be called by name.
A variable in the function definition.

topics.challenge: Write a function add(a, b) that returns the sum.
topics.challengeHint: Use 'return' to send a value back.

topics.title: Dictionaries
topics.explanation: Dictionaries store key-value pairs and allow for fast lookup by key.

keyTerms.definition: A collection of key-value pairs.
The identifier for a value in a dictionary.

commonMistakes: Using parentheses instead of curly braces.
topics.challenge: Create a dictionary for a book with keys: title and author.
topics.challengeHint: Use curly braces and key: value pairs.

topics.title: Classes
topics.explanation: Classes provide a way to create user-defined objects with data and functions.

keyTerms.definition: A template for creating objects.
An instance of a class.

topics.challenge: Create a class Car with attribute model.

topics.title: File IO
topics.explanation: File IO allows you to read from and write to files using built-in functions like open().

keyTerms.definition: An object representing a file.
Specifies reading or writing ('r', 'w').

topics.challenge: Write code to append 'Done' to a file 'tasks.txt'.
topics.challengeHint: Open file with mode 'a' for append.

topics.title: Modules
topics.explanation: Modules group reusable code. You import a module with 'import'.

keyTerms.definition: A file containing Python code.
Brings functions or classes into your script.

topics.challenge: Import the random module and print a random number between 1 and 10.

topics.title: Exceptions
topics.explanation: Exceptions are errors detected during execution. Use try/except to handle them.

keyTerms.definition: An event that disrupts normal program flow.
Block that catches and handles errors.

topics.challenge: Handle file not found when opening 'abc.txt'.

topics.title: APIs
topics.explanation: You can access web APIs using modules like 'requests' to fetch data from the internet.

keyTerms.definition: Application Programming Interface, a way for programs to talk to each other.
A message sent to a server to retrieve or send data.

topics.challenge: Fetch JSON data from a public API and print a value.

name: JavaScript
creator: Brendan Eich
description: JavaScript is the programming language of the web, primarily used to create interactive effects within browsers.
usedFor: Web development, front-end scripting, backend with Node.js.
funFact: JavaScript was created in just 10 days.

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables in JavaScript can be declared using var, let, or const. Use let or const for modern code.

keyTerms.definition: A container for storing data.
Creating a new variable.

commonMistakes: Using var when let/const is preferred.
topics.challenge: Declare a variable city with value 'Paris'.

topics.title: Arrays
topics.explanation: Arrays are used to store multiple values in a single variable.

keyTerms.definition: List-like object for storing sequences.
Numeric position of an item in an array.

commonMistakes: Trying to access out of bounds index.
topics.challenge: Make an array of 3 programming languages and print the first.

topics.title: Functions
topics.explanation: Functions are reusable blocks of code. Declare them using function or arrow syntax.

keyTerms.definition: Code you can call with arguments.
A variable to accept value in the function.

topics.challenge: Write a function sayHello that logs 'Hi!'.
topics.challengeHint: Use function keyword or arrow function.

topics.title: DOM
topics.explanation: The Document Object Model is a programming interface for web documents. You can interact with HTML using JavaScript.

keyTerms.definition: A tree representation of web page structure.
A single node in the DOM, like <div>.

topics.challenge: Change the background color of the body to yellow.

topics.title: Events
topics.explanation: Events let JavaScript respond to user actions like clicks or keypresses.

keyTerms.definition: An action by the user or browser.
A function that runs when an event occurs.

commonMistakes: Forgetting to pass function as argument.
topics.challenge: Log 'Button clicked' when a button is pressed.
topics.terminalNote: Run in browser with a button element

topics.title: Promises
topics.explanation: Promises handle asynchronous operations. They represent a value not available yet.

keyTerms.definition: An object for asynchronous results.
Handles resolved promise.

topics.challenge: Create a promise that resolves to 'Done'.

topics.title: Async Await
topics.explanation: 'async' and 'await' provide a way to write asynchronous code that looks synchronous.

keyTerms.definition: Marks a function as asynchronous.
Waits for promise resolution.

commonMistakes: Using await outside an async function.
topics.challenge: Write an async function that awaits 1 second then logs 'Waited'.

topics.title: Classes
topics.explanation: Classes provide a template for creating objects. Methods and properties are included.

keyTerms.definition: A blueprint for objects.
Method for initializing an object.

topics.challenge: Create a class Book with title property and a constructor.

topics.title: Modules
topics.explanation: Modules allow you to organize JavaScript in separate files and import/export pieces as needed.

keyTerms.definition: ES6 feature to encapsulate code in files.
Makes a variable/function accessible in other files.

codeExample: export function add(a, b) { return a + b }
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use '.js' in import path.
topics.challenge: Export a function greet() from a file and import it elsewhere.

topics.title: Fetch API
topics.explanation: Fetch API lets JavaScript get resources from servers via HTTP requests.

keyTerms.definition: A method to make network requests.
Used to handle async result of a fetch.

topics.challenge: Fetch JSON from an API and log a top-level key.

name: TypeScript
creator: Anders Hejlsberg
description: TypeScript adds optional static typing to JavaScript, enabling safer and more predictable code.
usedFor: Large-scale JavaScript applications, web development, tooling.
funFact: TypeScript compiles to plain JavaScript, so it can run anywhere JavaScript runs.

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables in TypeScript are declared with let, const, or var, but you can also provide a type annotation.

keyTerms.definition: Explicitly assigning a type to a variable.
Modern ways to declare variables.

commonMistakes: Assigning value not matching the type.
topics.challenge: Declare a variable 'active' of type boolean set to true.
topics.terminalNote: Compile with tsc file.ts then run node file.js

topics.title: Types
topics.explanation: TypeScript supports basic types like number, string, boolean, as well as custom types and interfaces.

keyTerms.definition: Built-in connected types, e.g. number, string.
Declaration of shape for objects.

solution: type Point = { x: number; y: number; };
topics.challenge: Define a Person interface with name:string and age:number.
topics.challengeHint: Start with interface then curly braces.

topics.title: Functions
topics.explanation: Functions can have typed parameters and return values to catch mistakes early.

keyTerms.definition: Declaration including parameter and return types.

codeExample: function add(a: number, b: number): number {
topics.challenge: Declare a function greet that takes a string and returns void.

topics.title: Interfaces
topics.explanation: Interfaces define the shape of objects, making code robust and self-documenting.

keyTerms.definition: Type describing object structure.
An object follows an interface shape.

solution: interface Animal { name: string; age: number }
topics.challenge: Define an interface Book with title:string.

topics.title: Classes
topics.explanation: TypeScript classes let you structure code using object-oriented programming with type safety.

keyTerms.definition: A user-defined type.
A property or method of a class.

codeExample: constructor(public x: number, public y: number) {}
solution: class User { name: string; constructor(name: string) { this.name = name } }
topics.challenge: Create a class Person with a private id:number.

topics.title: Generics
topics.explanation: Generics enable classes and functions to work with different types while maintaining type safety.

keyTerms.definition: A type variable for flexible functions/classes.

solution: function pair<T, U>(x: T, y: U): [T, U] { return [x, y]; }
topics.challenge: Write a generic function that returns its argument.
challengeSolution: function wrap<T>(val: T): T { return val; }

topics.title: Enums
topics.explanation: Enums allow you to define named constant values to use as types.

keyTerms.definition: A set of named constants.

commonMistakes: Using enums as strings by mistake.
topics.challenge: Define an enum Color with Red, Green, Blue.

topics.title: Type Aliases
topics.explanation: Type aliases assign a custom name to a type, making complex types easier to use.

keyTerms.definition: A custom name for a type.

solution: type Point = { x: number; y: number }
topics.challenge: Alias a type UserName as string.

topics.title: Modules
topics.explanation: Modules structure your program into self-contained units. Use export/import keywords.

keyTerms.definition: A file containing code you can export/import.
Makes code visible to other files.

codeExample: export function double(x: number) { return x * 2 }
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use .ts extension in imports.
topics.challenge: Export a constant PI from a separate file.
topics.terminalNote: Compile with tsc then run node main.js

topics.title: Type Guards
topics.explanation: Type guards are TypeScript features that narrow types using runtime checks to improve safety.

keyTerms.definition: A construct to test and narrow type at runtime.

codeExample: function isString(x: unknown): x is string {
solution: function isNum(x: any): x is number { return typeof x === 'number'; }
topics.challenge: Write a type guard to check if input is array.
challengeSolution: function isArray(x: any): x is any[] { return Array.isArray(x); }
